comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Verified in current checkout with one path correction. The checker lives at extensions/tau/scripts/check-runfork-usage.mjs, not repo-root scripts/. It hardcodes ALLOWED_FILES = { 'src/app.ts', 'src/agent/worker.ts', 'src/services/footer.ts' } (lines 4-8), so src/agent/worker.ts remains explicitly allowlisted even though that area has the worker lifecycle/runFork bug tracked separately. Detection is also literal substring matching only: line 42 checks line.includes('Effect.runFork('). That means aliases, imported renames, bracket access, wrapped helpers, or non-literal forms bypass the checker. Current tau source already mostly routes runFork through runtime adapters (e.g. worker.ts uses this.runFork(effect) rather than a direct literal), so the checker currently protects only direct textual Effect.runFork calls outside the allowlist. This is a real weakness in the guardrail, though it is a tooling/design issue rather than a runtime bug.
